summary: Verified the global SPA scroll-to-top fix in /app/frontend/src/App.js (AppRoutes useEffect on location.pathname + location.search, skipping when location.hash is truthy). Tested 8 navigation flows across KB, search, Community, Ecosystem, and in-page TOC anchors. All 8 scenarios pass — every route transition now lands at scrollY = 0, while in-page TOC anchor clicks (which use scrollIntoView without changing URL hash) correctly scroll to the section.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal, idiomatic, and correctly scoped. Dependency array [location.pathname, location.search] correctly covers both pathname navigations and query-string changes. The location.hash early-return correctly preserves anchor-based URLs and protects the existing OAuth callback handler (session_id in hash) which runs after the useEffect.

Note: Browser back-button navigation will also force scroll to top with this implementation. This is a minor regression from the typical SPA expectation of preserving scroll on back-nav. If users complain, consider tracking history action via useNavigationType() from react-router-dom and skipping scroll on POP actions. Not a blocker — the current fix matches the user-reported bug exactly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scroll-to-top fix verified working globally across SPA. No regressions found. TOC in-page anchors use scrollIntoView and do not change URL hash, so they coexist with the fix correctly. OAuth #session_id callback still handled because the hash check runs after the scroll useEffect (and the useEffect early-returns on any hash).
